project.name: alendadeghanor
project.description: A fan-made web app for creating and managing characters in the Brazilian TTRPG "A Lenda de Ghanor," featuring a guided character creation wizard, derived attribute calculation, printable character sheets, a full inventory and companion system, an arena mode for shared sessions, and AI-generated portraits backed by Supabase Auth/Database/Storage.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Directive for AI agents reminding them that this project uses a Next.js version with breaking changes and to consult the bundled docs before writing code.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview for 'A Lenda de Ghanor' character sheet app, covering setup, architecture, and feature highlights including the arena mode and AI portrait generation.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive AI-agent guide covering the full system architecture, database schema, game rules, server action patterns, inventory logic, and contributor checklists for the Ghanor character app.

nodes.name: ARENA_MODE_PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Technical design document for the Arena Mode feature, describing real-time shared session architecture, turn management, Supabase Realtime integration, and phased rollout plan.

nodes.name: PLANO_MAGIAS.md
nodes.summary: Design document for the spell system, outlining the magic school taxonomy, spell circle progression, mana cost rules, and database schema for storing spells per character.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Node.js package manifest defining Next.js 16.2.6/React 19 stack with Supabase, Zod, Zustand, react-hook-form, 3d-dice, and OpenAI as runtime dependencies, plus Vitest and Tailwind for dev.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration enabling strict mode, bundler module resolution, JSX react-jsx transform, and the `@/*` path alias used throughout the project.

nodes.name: next.config.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js configuration that adds a custom response header to serve the dice-box WASM file with the correct MIME type.

nodes.name: postcss.config.mjs
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration wiring up Tailwind CSS v4 via its dedicated PostCSS plugin.

nodes.name: eslint.config.mjs
nodes.summary: ESLint flat configuration combining Next.js core-web-vitals and TypeScript rule sets, with explicit ignores for build output directories.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest configuration setting up a jsdom test environment with global test APIs and the `@/*` path alias to match the project's TypeScript paths.

nodes.name: config.toml
nodes.summary: Supabase local development configuration covering API, database (Postgres 17), auth, storage, edge functions, and realtime settings for the alendadeghanor project.
nodes.languageNotes: TOML config file for the Supabase CLI; controls all local dev stack services including auth JWT secrets, storage backend, and SMTP settings.

nodes.name: manifest.json
nodes.summary: PWA web app manifest for 'Forja de Ghanor', defining name, theme colors, icons, and standalone display mode with pt-BR locale.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Core domain type definitions for the Ghanor rules engine, including Attribute, RaceId, ClassId, Size, ArmorId, ShieldId, CharacterBuild, and display-label maps.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses TypeScript const assertions and mapped types to derive string-literal union types from arrays, ensuring compile-time exhaustiveness.

nodes.name: rules.ts
nodes.summary: Pure game-rules engine implementing HP/MP/defense calculations, attribute derivation from race/origin/mutation bonuses, skill bonuses, and class skill validation — all without I/O.

nodes.name: getFinalAttributes
nodes.summary: Computes final character attributes by summing base attributes with racial modifiers, human/half-elf racial choices, aberrant mutation modifiers, and origin attribute bonuses.

nodes.name: calculateHp
nodes.summary: Calculates maximum HP for a character at a given level by combining class base HP, Constitution modifier per level, racial HP bonuses, and origin HP bonuses.

nodes.name: calculateMp
nodes.summary: Calculates maximum MP by combining class MP per level, racial/origin bonuses, caster key-attribute bonus for bardo/clerigo/mago, and ascetic mutation bonus.

nodes.name: calculateDefense
nodes.summary: Computes character Defense as 10 + Dex modifier + armor bonus + shield bonus + origin/mutation defense bonuses.

nodes.name: calculateSkillBonus
nodes.summary: Computes total skill bonus as half-level + attribute modifier + training bonus + flat racial/origin bonuses for a given skill ID.

nodes.name: getRequiredClassSkills
nodes.summary: Returns the mandatory trained skills for a class, including fixed class skills plus optional combat skill choice for bucaneiro/cacador/soldado and social skill choice for nobre.

nodes.name: leveling.ts
nodes.summary: Game leveling system implementing tier progression, HP/MP gain per level, spell circle unlocking, multiclass support, and attribute increase validation — the core level-up engine.

nodes.name: computeLevelUp
nodes.summary: Primary level-up computation: calculates new HP/MP maximums, handles Con retroactive HP adjustment, tracks class levels for multiclassing, detects tier changes, and identifies new spell circle unlocks.

nodes.name: canIncreaseAttribute
nodes.summary: Validates whether a given attribute can be increased at the next level by checking that the same attribute has not already been increased in the current tier.

nodes.name: computeSkillRollModifier
nodes.summary: Calculates a detailed breakdown of a skill roll modifier (half-level, attribute mod, training bonus) for display in the character sheet roll modal.

nodes.name: classes.ts
nodes.summary: Static data registry for all 11 playable classes defining HP base, MP per level, key attribute, fixed/choosable skills, proficiencies, and first-level abilities; exported as a lookup map.

nodes.name: races.ts
nodes.summary: Static data registry for all 7 playable races including attribute modifiers, movement, skill bonuses, HP/MP bonuses per level, and the 10-option aberrant mutation catalog.

nodes.name: origins.ts
nodes.summary: Static data registry of 30 character origins, each providing trained skills, stat bonuses (HP/MP/defense/movement), starting items, and a generated slug ID derived from the origin's name.

nodes.name: skills.ts
nodes.summary: Static data registry of 27 skills mapping each skill to its governing attribute, and flags for armor penalty and trained-only restriction; exported as a lookup map.

nodes.name: inventory.ts
nodes.summary: Pure-logic module covering all inventory mechanics: currency conversion (PC/PP/PO), carry capacity, encumbrance zones, item pricing with improvements and Arcanium, armor and weapon proficiency checks, defense and movement calculation with equipment, armor penalty propagation to skills, and per-class starter kit definitions.

nodes.name: toPc
nodes.summary: Converts gold (PO), silver (PP), and copper (PC) denominations into the canonical internal currency value in PC.

nodes.name: fromPc
nodes.summary: Decomposes a raw PC value back into { po, pp, pc } denominations for display or arithmetic.

nodes.name: formatMoney
nodes.summary: Formats a PC amount into a human-readable string like '1 PO 2 PP 3 PC', omitting zero-valued denominations.

nodes.name: carryCapacity
nodes.summary: Calculates character carry capacity in spaces from Strength modifier, applying the rule-book formula (10 + 2×STR for positive, 10 + STR for negative).

nodes.name: computeDefenseWithEquipment
nodes.summary: Computes total Defense from Dexterity modifier, equipped armor and shield bonuses, overload penalty, and any other bonus, returning the total, armor penalty, and a human-readable breakdown string.

nodes.name: appliesArmorPenaltyToSkill
nodes.summary: Determines whether armor penalty applies to a given skill, accounting for skill type, proficiency in the worn armor and shield, and the fixed penalty-skill list.

nodes.name: getStarterItems
nodes.summary: Returns the list of item slugs from a class-specific starter kit, choosing martial/heavy/shield gear based on character proficiency flags.

nodes.name: animals.ts
nodes.summary: Defines all companion-related types (CompanionKind, CompanionType, PowerLevel, Companion) and the static catalog of purchasable animal companions (ANIMAL_CONFIGS) with their display-label lookup tables.

nodes.name: names.ts
nodes.summary: Provides procedural character name generation from race-specific phoneme tables, with an optional random title suffix, used during character creation.

nodes.name: randomCharacterName
nodes.summary: Generates a single random character name for a given race by combining a start syllable, an end syllable, and an optional title from static tables.

nodes.name: quiz.ts
nodes.summary: Defines all types and the full static question bank (18 questions across 3 acts) for the character-creation personality quiz that maps player preferences to RPG attributes, class scores, skill points, and personality tags.
nodes.languageNotes: All question payloads encode multi-dimensional score vectors (attrs, classes, skills, tags) used by quiz-engine.ts for character generation.

nodes.name: quiz-engine.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the quiz scoring engine that aggregates quiz answers into attribute distributions, selects the best-fit class and origin, fills trained skills, and generates a one-sentence character concept string with gender concordance.

nodes.name: computeCharacter
nodes.summary: Core quiz-to-character pipeline: aggregates answer payloads, distributes attribute points via point-buy rules, ranks classes and origins by weighted scores, assigns trained skills, selects starting equipment, and produces a localized concept string.

nodes.name: spells.ts
nodes.summary: Defines all spell types and the complete multi-circle spell catalog for all caster classes, plus lookup helpers (by id, by name, by class, by circle) and a caster-class predicate.

nodes.name: getSpellsForClass
nodes.summary: Filters the full spell catalog to spells available to a given class.

nodes.name: getSpellsForClassAndCircle
nodes.summary: Filters the spell catalog to spells available to a class up to a maximum circle level, used for level-gated spell selection.

nodes.name: powers.ts
nodes.summary: Defines all power types and the complete catalog of general, combat, class, origin, and race powers, with helpers for lookup by id/name, filtering by class, and the per-class starting power map.

nodes.name: getPowersForClass
nodes.summary: Returns all powers accessible to a class: its own class powers plus all general and combat powers.

nodes.name: shop.ts
nodes.summary: Defines shop stages and merchant-type configurations, and implements inventory generation logic that filters the item catalog by stage constraints, merchant categories, price caps, and DM mode, as well as price adjustment per settlement stage.

nodes.name: generateShopInventory
nodes.summary: Generates a slug-to-quantity inventory map for a given stage and merchant type by applying stage-level filters, price caps, category restrictions, and stock multipliers.

nodes.name: shop-stages.ts
nodes.summary: Exports a read-only STAGE_INFO array with display labels, icons, and descriptions for each of the five settlement-size shop stages.

nodes.name: portrait.ts
nodes.summary: Provides race and class descriptor strings and the buildPortraitPrompt helper that assembles an AI image-generation prompt from a character's race, class, appearance notes, age, and concept.

nodes.name: buildPortraitPrompt
nodes.summary: Assembles a structured natural-language prompt for AI portrait generation by combining race/class descriptors with optional appearance, age, and concept text.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Re-exports a single cn() utility that merges Tailwind class names using clsx and tailwind-merge, used throughout UI components.

nodes.name: username.ts
nodes.summary: Provides username normalization and validation utilities for the app's username-based auth flow, converting a display username to a synthetic email address accepted by Supabase Auth.

nodes.name: use-dm-mode.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that persists and toggles Dungeon Master mode per character via localStorage, exposing isActive, toggle, setActive, and a hydration flag for SSR safety.

nodes.name: useDmMode
nodes.summary: Custom React hook managing DM mode state per character via localStorage with hydration safety and toggle/set helpers.

nodes.name: useTilt.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that applies a 3D CSS perspective tilt effect to a div element on mouse move and resets on mouse leave, used for card hover animations.

nodes.name: useTilt
nodes.summary: Provides onMouseMove and onMouseLeave handlers that apply perspective-based CSS tilt and shadow to the referenced element.

nodes.name: character.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the Zod schema and inferred type for the character creation/edit form, enforcing attribute ranges, required fields, and array defaults for skills, powers, and spells.

nodes.name: server.ts
nodes.summary: Factory that creates a cookie-authenticated Supabase client for use in Server Components and Server Actions, respecting Row-Level Security policies.

nodes.name: admin.ts
nodes.summary: Factory that creates a service-role Supabase client that bypasses RLS, intended for privileged server actions that need unrestricted database access.

nodes.name: client.ts
nodes.summary: Factory that creates a browser-side Supabase client using the SSR helper, intended for client components requiring authenticated database access.

nodes.name: 3d-dice__dice-box.d.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript ambient module declaration for the @3d-dice/dice-box library, exposing DiceBox class, DiceBoxConfig, and DieResult interfaces for the 3D dice roller feature.

nodes.name: ghanor_rules.json
nodes.summary: Comprehensive machine-readable rulebook extract for A Lenda de Ghanor (7164 lines) covering attributes, skills, classes, races, combat, magic, equipment, and all core game mechanics, used as a reference for AI-assisted features and rule validation.

nodes.name: characters
nodes.summary: Initial definition of the characters table, establishing the core entity for player character data including attributes, race, class, origin, equipment, and portrait fields, along with RLS policies and an updated_at trigger.

nodes.name: character-portraits-bucket
nodes.summary: Creates the public Supabase Storage bucket for AI-generated character portrait images.

nodes.name: items
nodes.summary: Defines the global items catalog table with all weapon, armor, and item metadata fields, plus the enums for item categories, weapon attributes, armor categories, and inventory locations.

nodes.name: character_inventory
nodes.summary: Defines the character_inventory join table that links characters to items, tracking location (equipped/worn/carried/storage/mount/sold), improvements, Arcanium enchantment, and custom item data.

nodes.name: money_transactions
nodes.summary: Defines the money_transactions table for auditing all financial changes (purchases, sales, gifts) for each character, storing the balance after each transaction in copper pieces.

nodes.name: shop_inventories
nodes.summary: Adds the shop_inventories table for dynamically generated per-character shop stock by stage and merchant type, and enables RLS on the items catalog table for authenticated reads.

nodes.name: level_ups
nodes.summary: Defines the level_ups table tracking every class level gained per character, including HP/MP gained, powers chosen, spells learned, and multiclass history, with RLS policies.

nodes.name: rolls
nodes.summary: Defines the rolls table for persisting all dice roll history per character, storing each roll's modifier breakdown, natural result, CD, outcome (success/failure/crit), and roll mode (standard/opposed/aided).

nodes.name: arenas
nodes.summary: Defines the arenas and arena_participants tables for real-time shared combat sessions, with DM-scoped and participant-scoped RLS policies and Supabase Realtime publication for live HP/MP sync.

nodes.name: companions
nodes.summary: Defines the companions and vehicles tables for tracking animal companions, mercenaries, and transport owned by a character, and migrates existing animal/vehicle inventory entries into the new tables.

nodes.name: shop_sessions
nodes.summary: Creates the shop_sessions table for short-lived (10-minute TTL) shared shop item lists generated per stage, accessible to all authenticated users.

nodes.name: schema.sql
nodes.summary: Initial database schema defining the characters table, Storage bucket for portraits, RLS policies, and an updated_at trigger function for the core character entity.

nodes.name: migrate_equipment.sql
nodes.summary: Migration 004 that introduces the full equipment system: items catalog table, character_inventory table, money_transactions table, currency enums, and the money_to_pc SQL helper function.

nodes.name: migrate_equipment_v2.sql
nodes.summary: Migration 005 that adds RLS to the items catalog, adds min_stage and location_details columns, expands acquired_from constraints, creates the shop_inventories table, and corrects orphaned inventory entries.

nodes.name: migrate_leveling_and_rolls.sql
nodes.summary: Migration that adds current_level and class_levels columns to characters, creates the level_ups and rolls tables, and installs the sync_current_level trigger to keep current_level derived from class_levels JSONB.

nodes.name: seed_items_weapons.sql
nodes.summary: Idempotent seed populating 53 weapons from the Lenda de Ghanor rulebook (simple, martial, and exotic weapons plus ammunition), with full combat stats for each weapon.

nodes.name: seed_items_general.sql
nodes.summary: Idempotent seed populating armors, shields, adventure equipment, tools, clothing, esoteric items, alchemical preparations, poisons, animals, and services (approximately 80 items).

nodes.name: seed_items_catalog.sql
nodes.summary: Expanded catalog seed (Prompt 4/4) adding ~128 items across all categories including bens_comuns, alquimico_catalisador, alquimia_mistica, vehicles, magic items, and additional clothing, tools, esoteric, poison, and service entries.

nodes.name: migrate_arena.sql
nodes.summary: Migration creating the Arena mode tables (arenas and arena_participants) for shared real-time combat sessions, with DM and participant RLS policies and Supabase Realtime publication setup.

nodes.name: migrate_companions.sql
nodes.summary: Migration creating companions and vehicles tables, adding a companion_id FK to character_inventory, and migrating existing animal and vehicle inventory entries into the new dedicated tables.

nodes.name: migrate_cosmetic.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding the is_cosmetic column to items and marking specific clothing and containers (traje_viajante, bolsa_lona, mochila, etc.) as cosmetic, then correcting misplaced inventory locations.

nodes.name: migrate_equip_flags.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding can_be_held, can_be_worn, and is_two_handed boolean columns to items, then bulk-setting them by category (weapons, shields, armors, clothing, esoteric, alchemicals) and correcting misplaced inventory slots.

nodes.name: migrate_purchasable.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding the is_purchasable flag to items, marking non-commercial starter weapons (bordao, tacape, funda) as non-purchasable, and re-applying equip flags for items inserted after migrate_equip_flags ran.

nodes.name: migrate_shop_sessions.sql
nodes.summary: Migration creating the shop_sessions table for anonymous shared shop item lists with a 10-minute TTL, accessible to all authenticated users for reading and session creators for writing.

nodes.name: migrate_spells_powers_slugs.sql
nodes.summary: Migration adding the active_effects column to characters, creating GIN indexes on spells/powers/active_effects arrays, and normalizing existing spell/power names to slug format (lowercase, no accents, underscores).

nodes.name: seed.sql
nodes.summary: Consolidated seed file combining all three item seed files plus post-seed UPDATE statements applying equip flags, cosmetic flags, purchasable flags, and magic item wearability — used for local Supabase seeding.

nodes.name: 00000000000000_initial_schema.sql
nodes.summary: Authoritative idempotent full schema combining all migrations into a single file for local development: creates all tables (characters, items, companions, vehicles, character_inventory, money_transactions, level_ups, rolls, shop_inventories, shop_sessions, arenas, arena_participants), enums, helper functions, triggers, indexes, RLS policies, Storage bucket, and Realtime publication.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses idempotent patterns (IF NOT EXISTS, ON CONFLICT, DROP POLICY IF EXISTS) so it can be re-run safely in local Supabase CLI reset workflows.

nodes.name: audit-catalog.js
nodes.summary: CLI script that parses all three item seed SQL files and produces a per-category coverage report comparing found item counts against expected totals (255 items across 16 categories), flagging duplicates and unexpected categories.

nodes.name: validate-seed.js
nodes.summary: Pre-build validation script that parses all three item seed SQL files, verifying slug format, category validity, required weapon fields, armor bonus presence, expected per-category item counts, duplicate slugs, and presence of all 50 mandatory combat weapon slugs — exits non-zero on any error.

nodes.name: globals.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet that imports Tailwind v4, defines CSS custom properties for the app's warm parchment color palette, safe-area insets, bottom-nav height, touch-friendly defaults, and utility classes for mobile layout and page-enter animations.

nodes.name: inventory.test.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Vitest test suite for the inventory module, covering currency conversion (toPc/fromPc/formatMoney), carry-capacity rules, item-space totals, overload detection, worn-item limits, pricing with improvements and Arcanium, defense and movement calculations, armor-penalty skills, and per-class proficiency flags.

nodes.name: quiz.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite that validates the quiz-engine's class recommendation logic by feeding each of the 11 playable classes an ideal answer set and asserting that class is ranked first in the suggested results.

nodes.name: quiz-engine.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration-level Vitest suite for computeCharacter, verifying that warrior-focused answers produce melee classes with high STR/CON and that magic-focused answers produce a mago character with high INT and correct skills.

nodes.name: rules.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite covering the core rules module: attribute roll conversion, final-attribute computation across all races, HP/MP calculation across all classes, defense, movement, and trained-skill bonus.

nodes.name: shop.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite for the shop module, testing stage-based inventory generation (stage filtering, blacksmith vs. general merchant stock, DM-mode full catalog), stage price multipliers, and rare-item unlock flags.

nodes.name: classes.test.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal Vitest smoke test that asserts the classes module exposes exactly 11 classes and that key class stats (mago hpBase, clerigo mpPerLevel) are correct.

nodes.name: races.test.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal Vitest smoke test confirming 7 playable races, a specific racial property (gigante size), and 10 aberrant mutations are defined.

nodes.name: spells.test.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal Vitest smoke test ensuring the spells module provides at least 8 first-circle spells and includes entries for mago and clerigo classes.

nodes.name: powers.test.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal Vitest smoke test verifying the powers module is non-empty and that a specific combat power (ataque_poderoso) has the correct type.

nodes.name: skills.test.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal Vitest smoke test confirming all 27 skills from the game's Table 2-1 are present, with correct attribute mapping and trained-only flags.

nodes.name: origins.test.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal Vitest smoke test asserting at least 28 origins are defined and that specific origin bonuses (acolito mpPerLevelBonus, escudeiro defenseBonus) are correct.

nodes.name: names.test.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal Vitest smoke test for the names module, verifying deterministic race-flavored name generation and batch name option lists.

nodes.name: types.test.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal Vitest smoke test confirming the six core TTRPG attributes array and their Portuguese-abbreviated labels are exported correctly from the types module.

nodes.name: button.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable Button component with four Tailwind-styled variants (primary, secondary, ghost, danger) and three sizes (default, lg, icon), using the amber/stone color palette consistent with the app's parchment theme.

nodes.name: card.tsx
nodes.summary: Exports two presentational layout primitives: Card (a rounded amber-tinted container) and SectionTitle (a bold heading with optional icon slot), used throughout character-sheet tabs.

nodes.name: input.tsx
nodes.summary: Exports styled Input and Textarea form primitives with consistent amber-focus ring and 16 px text size to prevent iOS auto-zoom, used across forms in the character sheet and wizard.

nodes.name: item-icon.tsx
nodes.summary: Client component exporting three icon primitives — ItemIcon (SVG image with Lucide fallback by category), ClassIcon (SVG image with graceful hide on error), and RaceIcon (Lucide icon mapped from race id) — used wherever items, classes, or races are displayed.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses progressive image fallback: tries slug-specific SVG paths first, then falls back to a Lucide category icon, avoiding broken image placeholders.

nodes.name: app-shell.tsx
nodes.summary: Async server component that wraps page content and conditionally renders the BottomNav only when a Supabase session is active, acting as the authenticated app chrome.

nodes.name: bottom-nav.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side sticky bottom navigation bar with four links (Home, Characters, Arena, Create) that highlights the active route and self-hides on auth, wizard, and character-sheet screens.

nodes.name: brand-logo.tsx
nodes.summary: Presentational BrandLogo component rendering the app's Swords icon and 'A Lenda de Ghanor' wordmark in three size variants, used on the landing page and auth screens.

nodes.name: page-container.tsx
nodes.summary: Layout wrapper that constrains content to a max-width of 2xl, applies safe-area padding, and optionally reserves space for the bottom navigation bar or uses compact padding for wizard flows.

nodes.name: page-header.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable page header component with a large title, optional subtitle, optional back-navigation link with arrow icon, and an optional right-aligned slot for action buttons.

nodes.name: page-transition.tsx
nodes.summary: Exports two client components: NavigationProgress (an animated amber top progress bar driven by click and pathname events) and PageTransitionLayout (a wrapper that triggers the page-enter CSS animation on each route change).

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Root Next.js layout wrapping the entire application with PWA metadata, viewport settings, and global shell components including navigation progress and page transitions.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Home page server component that shows a marketing landing page for unauthenticated users and a character creation mode selector (guided vs. full) for authenticated users.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Login page wrapping the AuthForm component in login mode with a branded layout and a link to the signup page.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Signup page wrapping the AuthForm component in signup mode with a branded layout and a link to the login page.

nodes.name: actions.ts
nodes.summary: Server action providing the logout function that signs out the current user via Supabase and redirects to the home page.

nodes.name: logout
nodes.summary: Server action that signs out the current Supabase session and redirects the user to the root path.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: OAuth callback route handler that exchanges an authorization code for a Supabase session and redirects authenticated users to the characters list.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route handling username-based account creation using the Supabase admin client, including Zod validation, username-to-email conversion, and friendly error normalization.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the admin client to bypass Supabase email confirmation flow, enabling username-only registration without SMTP setup.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Handles POST requests for user signup: validates input schema, converts username to internal email, creates the user via Supabase admin API, and returns normalized error messages.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route that generates an AI character portrait via Pollinations FLUX model, enforces an 8-hour cooldown per character, uploads the result to Supabase Storage, and saves the public URL to the character record.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Pollinations.ai (free, no API key) with a 90-second AbortSignal timeout; portrait is stored in the 'character-portraits' Supabase Storage bucket.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Validates portrait generation request, checks authentication and ownership, enforces cooldown, calls Pollinations AI, uploads the image to Supabase Storage, and updates the character record with the portrait URL.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route returning six randomly generated character names for a given race, used by the character creation wizard to suggest names.

nodes.name: auth-form.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side authentication form supporting both login and signup modes, managing form state with React hooks, calling the signup API route, and signing in via Supabase client.

nodes.name: AuthForm
nodes.summary: Reusable form component for login and signup flows, handling submission via useTransition, showing inline error messages, and redirecting to the characters page on success.

nodes.name: logout-button.tsx
nodes.summary: Client component rendering a logout button that triggers the server-side logout action via useTransition, with configurable variant and class props.

nodes.name: LogoutButton
nodes.summary: Button component that calls the logout server action inside a React transition, disabling itself while pending.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Characters list page that fetches the authenticated user's characters from Supabase and renders each as a card with portrait, class icon, level, and derived stats (HP, MP, defense).

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Full character creation page that renders the CharacterWizard component inside a page layout for the manual, step-by-step character creation flow.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Guided character creation page that renders the GuidedCreationWizard for a question-driven character building experience aimed at new players.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Character sheet page that parallelizes fetching of character data, level-up history, inventory, money transactions, item catalog, and companions before rendering the full CharacterSheet component.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Promise.all for parallel data fetching and filters out animal/vehicle items from the catalog passed to the inventory modal.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Character edit page fetching the character's narrative fields from Supabase and rendering the EditCharacterForm for name, concept, appearance, and backstory updates.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Level-up page that loads character data and level history, guards against leveling past 20, and passes a complete character snapshot to the LevelUpWizard component.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Shop route page that loads the character's money and active shop session before rendering the ShopPage component for in-game item purchasing.

nodes.name: actions.ts
nodes.summary: Server actions for character lifecycle management: creating characters from wizard state (both full and guided modes), updating narrative fields, deleting characters, and granting the automated starter kit with randomized starting money.
nodes.languageNotes: The saveCharacter and saveGuidedCharacter actions call rules.ts functions (calculateHp, calculateMp, calculateDefense) to derive stats at creation time; starter kit uses crypto.randomInt for d6 rolls.

nodes.name: grantStarterKit
nodes.summary: Grants a class-specific starter kit to a newly created character: looks up item slugs in the catalog, inserts inventory rows, rolls 4d6 PP for starting money, and records the transaction.

nodes.name: saveCharacter
nodes.summary: Server action that creates a new character from the full wizard state: derives final attributes and stats using game rules, inserts the characters row, and triggers starter kit granting.

nodes.name: updateCharacter
nodes.summary: Server action that updates narrative fields (name, concept, age, appearance, personality, history, objective) on an existing owned character, revalidating both the character and list pages.

nodes.name: deleteCharacter
nodes.summary: Server action that deletes a character record and revalidates the characters list path.

nodes.name: saveGuidedCharacter
nodes.summary: Server action that creates a new character from the guided wizard flow: computes build and stats from the guided answers' computed object, records the creation mode and quiz answers, then grants the starter kit.

nodes.name: character-sheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Root character sheet component that orchestrates all sub-tabs (sheet, inventory, companions), renders derived stats, HP/MP trackers persisted to localStorage, skill roll dialogs, DM-mode editing panel, portrait upload/AI-generation, and level-up toasts.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of useTransition for non-blocking server action calls; HP/MP state is client-local via localStorage with max values sourced from the database row.

nodes.name: CharacterSheet
nodes.summary: Main exported component that renders the full character sheet with tabbed navigation, DM editing panel, vital trackers, attribute grid, skill rolls, attacks, spells, and portrait management.

nodes.name: VitalTracker
nodes.summary: Interactive HP/MP tracker card with +1/-1/+5/-5 buttons and tap-to-edit direct input, displaying a progress bar.

nodes.name: ActiveEffectsCard
nodes.summary: Displays the list of active spell/power effects with individual dismiss buttons.

nodes.name: inventory-tab.tsx
nodes.summary: Full inventory management tab with sub-tabs for equipped, carried, stored, and history views; handles item movement, selling, consuming, money adjustment, and DM-mode item editing (improvements, Arcanium, notes, deletion).

nodes.name: companions-tab.tsx
nodes.summary: Companions management tab showing animals, mercenaries, class companions, and followers with inline editing, HP tracking, mark-as-dead, and an add-companion modal supporting animal purchase or custom entry.

nodes.name: CompanionsTab
nodes.summary: Exported main component that groups companions by kind, shows capacity summary, and coordinates the add-companion modal and individual CompanionCard children.

nodes.name: attacks-section.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders equipped weapon cards with computed attack and damage modifiers, proficiency checks, and roll dialogs; each card has a 3D tilt effect and expandable detail panel.

nodes.name: AttacksSection
nodes.summary: Exported component that renders a grid of WeaponCard items and manages the shared RollDialog state.

nodes.name: WeaponCard
nodes.summary: Individual weapon card showing attack bonus, damage, critical, and action buttons for rolling attack and damage; collapses/expands detailed stats.

nodes.name: computeAttackMod
nodes.summary: Pure function computing total attack modifier and human-readable breakdown from skill bonus and proficiency status.

nodes.name: spells-section.tsx
nodes.summary: Spells and powers section component with cards for each spell/power grouped by effect/element/activation type; supports PM cost tracking, roll dialogs with hit effects, use-per-rest counters, and DM-mode add/remove.

nodes.name: journey-section.tsx
nodes.summary: Collapsible timeline component showing level-up history with details about HP/MP gained, powers chosen, spells learned, and attribute increases per level.

nodes.name: JourneySection
nodes.summary: Exported component rendering a collapsible timeline of level-up events using class names and tier labels.

nodes.name: edit-character-form.tsx
nodes.summary: Form component for editing character narrative fields (name, concept, age, appearance, personality, history, objective) and submitting via the updateCharacter server action.

nodes.name: EditCharacterForm
nodes.summary: Exported form component that manages controlled state for all narrative fields and submits them through the updateCharacter action.

nodes.name: portrait-confirm-dialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Modal dialog that previews the AI portrait generation prompts (race, class, appearance descriptors) and asks for confirmation before calling the generation endpoint.

nodes.name: PortraitConfirmDialog
nodes.summary: Exported modal that shows the prompts to be sent for AI portrait generation with cancel/confirm actions.

nodes.name: ConsumeEffect.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-screen CSS-animation overlay displayed when a consumable item is used, with particle effects, glows, and category-specific emoji and colors configurable by ConsumeType.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses injected CSS keyframe strings with inline styles rather than Tailwind for precise animation timing control tied to DURATION constants.

nodes.name: ConsumeEffect
nodes.summary: Exported component rendering the full-screen consumption animation overlay with particles, glow, emoji, and text for the given item category.

nodes.name: getConsumeType
nodes.summary: Pure utility mapping an item category string to its ConsumeType variant for animation configuration.

nodes.name: add-item-modal.tsx
nodes.summary: Modal for adding items to a character's inventory from the catalog (with search/filter, quantity, location, improvements, Arcanium), as a custom item, or as a quick free-form item; supports DM-mode bypass of costs.

nodes.name: AddItemModal
nodes.summary: Exported modal component with three tabs (catalog, custom, quick) for adding items to inventory, managing all form state and submission to server actions.

nodes.name: dm-mode-banner.tsx
nodes.summary: Small banner/toggle component showing whether DM mode is active; renders a subtle activation button when off or an indigo banner with pulsing indicator when on.

nodes.name: DmModeBanner
nodes.summary: Exported component that toggles between a minimal activation button and a prominent DM-mode indicator banner.

nodes.name: ShopPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive shop page component with timed session management, category filters, item search, cart with improvements/Arcanium options, countdown timer, and purchase submission via server actions.

nodes.name: ShopPage
nodes.summary: Exported main shop component that manages session state, cart, filters, and checkout flow with a live session countdown timer.

nodes.name: character-wizard.tsx
nodes.summary: Multi-step manual character creation wizard (8 steps) for choosing name, attributes, race, class, origin, skills, spells, and equipment; uses Zustand store for cross-step state and submits via the saveCharacter action.

nodes.name: CharacterWizard
nodes.summary: Exported root component of the manual character creation wizard, orchestrating all 8 wizard steps with navigation, validation, and state management.

nodes.name: store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store definition for the character creation wizard, holding all draft character state (name, attributes, race, class, origin, skills, powers, spells, equipment, story fields) with step navigation and patch update actions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zustand create() pattern; the store is a singleton shared across all wizard steps without React context.

nodes.name: LevelUpWizard.tsx
nodes.summary: Multi-step level-up wizard that lets the player choose a class for the new level (with optional multiclass), pick a power, select new spells if a caster, and optionally increase an attribute; saves via the saveLevelUp server action and triggers the celebration animation.

nodes.name: LevelUpWizard
nodes.summary: Exported level-up wizard component managing step state, class/power/spell/attribute selections, preview computation, and server submission with celebration overlay.

nodes.name: LevelUpCelebration.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-screen canvas-based confetti celebration overlay displayed after a successful level-up, animating multiple particle bursts and auto-dismissing after 2.4 seconds.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses requestAnimationFrame with a hand-written particle physics loop on an HTML canvas; draws circles, stars, and rectangles using CanvasRenderingContext2D.

nodes.name: LevelUpCelebration
nodes.summary: Exported canvas confetti component that fires multi-burst particle animations and invokes onDone after 2.4 s.

nodes.name: GuidedCreationWizard.tsx
nodes.summary: Orchestrator for the guided (quiz-based) character creation flow, managing steps: welcome, race selection, 18-question quiz with act transitions, final touches, and result with character saving.

nodes.name: GuidedCreationWizard
nodes.summary: Exported orchestrator component routing between guided wizard sub-steps and accumulating quiz answers, race choices, and character touches.

nodes.name: QuizWelcome.tsx
nodes.summary: Welcome screen for the guided quiz, explaining the 18-question personality quiz and providing a start button.

nodes.name: QuizActTransition.tsx
nodes.summary: Auto-advancing interstitial screen shown between quiz acts, displaying thematic transitional text and automatically calling onDone after 2 seconds.

nodes.name: QuizQuestion.tsx
nodes.summary: Individual quiz question screen with progress bar, act label, scene text, prompt, and four selectable answer options plus back/skip navigation.

nodes.name: QuizRaceSelect.tsx
nodes.summary: Race selection step in the guided wizard; renders race cards with modifiers, handles race-specific sub-choices (human +1 attributes, half-elf bonus, aberrant mutations), and includes a random-pick option.

nodes.name: QuizRaceSelect
nodes.summary: Exported component managing race selection with conditional sub-choice panels and validation before advancing.

nodes.name: QuizResult.tsx
nodes.summary: Final step of the guided wizard showing the computed class suggestions and origin recommendations, allowing the player to select class/origin, choose starter spells and powers, then save the character via saveGuidedCharacter.

nodes.name: QuizResult
nodes.summary: Exported result component displaying suggested classes and origins, managing final spell/power selection, and submitting the guided character to the database.

nodes.name: QuizTouches.tsx
nodes.summary: Final personal details form in the guided quiz (gender, name with random generator, age, appearance, objective) before computing and displaying the character result.

nodes.name: QuizTouches
nodes.summary: Exported form component collecting name, age, appearance, objective, and gender before submitting to the quiz result computation.

nodes.name: ArenaBanner.tsx
nodes.summary: Client component that fetches and displays a banner linking a character to their current active arena session, shown on the character sheet.

nodes.name: ArenaCreateForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Client form component for the Dungeon Master to create a new arena session with a name, calling the createArena server action and redirecting to the new arena.

nodes.name: ArenaDashboard.tsx
nodes.summary: DM-facing arena dashboard component with Supabase Realtime subscription, showing a live grid of player cards and controls to close the session, add NPCs, and open the DmActionDrawer.

nodes.name: ArenaJoinFlow.tsx
nodes.summary: Client component for visitors to browse their characters and join an existing arena session by selecting a character and calling joinArena.

nodes.name: ArenaJoinForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Simple form component allowing a player to enter an arena token and navigate to the arena page without any server action calls.

nodes.name: ArenaPlayerCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Displays a participant card in the arena dashboard with HP/MP progress bars, health-status color coding, tier label, and an actions button that opens the DmActionDrawer.

nodes.name: ArenaPlayerView.tsx
nodes.summary: Player-facing arena view with Supabase Realtime updates showing the player's own HP/MP and a read-only health bar for each other participant, with a leave button.

nodes.name: ArenaTokenShare.tsx
nodes.summary: Utility component that displays the arena token prominently and provides copy-to-clipboard and native share API buttons.

nodes.name: DmActionDrawer.tsx
nodes.summary: Complex bottom-sheet drawer giving the DM full control over a selected participant, with tabbed panels for adjusting HP/MP/attributes, giving or taking items from the catalog or custom, and distributing or charging money.

nodes.name: StatControl
nodes.summary: Reusable sub-component rendering a labeled stat bar with quick-delta buttons and a direct-value input for PV or PM adjustment.

nodes.name: DmActionDrawer
nodes.summary: Main exported drawer component orchestrating all DM actions (HP, MP, money, items, attributes, remove participant) with tabbed navigation and debounced item search.

nodes.name: Dice20.tsx
nodes.summary: Animated d20 die visual component that displays rolling, critical, and fumble states with CSS keyframe animations and accessibility aria-live attributes.

nodes.name: HitEffect.tsx
nodes.summary: Visual hit-effect overlay component supporting 12 damage types (slash, arrow, fire, ice, lightning, acid, sonic, necro, holy, dark, psychic, magic) rendered as CSS-animated SVG and div layers.
nodes.languageNotes: All keyframes are inlined as a style tag to avoid global CSS pollution in Next.js.

nodes.name: HitEffect
nodes.summary: Exported component that selects the correct per-type effect renderer, runs the duration timer, and calls onDone when the animation completes.

nodes.name: RollDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-screen 3D dice roller dialog backed by the dice-box WebGL library, featuring seven visual skins, crit/fumble detection, modifier display, and an optional hit-effect animation on close.
nodes.languageNotes: dice-box is dynamically imported via Function('return import(...)') to sidestep circular-import issues with webpack.

nodes.name: RollDialog
nodes.summary: Main exported dialog managing dice-box lifecycle, skin selection persisted to localStorage, rolling state, and result rendering.

nodes.name: useDiceRoller.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook managing the d20 animation lifecycle (idle → rolling → landed) with a configurable duration and a timer-based phase transition.

nodes.name: useDiceRoller
nodes.summary: Hook exporting startRoll, reset, phase, and naturalRoll to drive dice animation state from server-provided results.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Arena index server page that authenticates the user, lists their active arenas, and renders create/join forms alongside a list of existing arena links.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Layout wrapper for all arena routes, applying the amber radial-gradient background and PageContainer with bottom navigation.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Dynamic arena token server page that determines the user's role (DM, participant, visitor) and renders the appropriate component: ArenaDashboard, ArenaPlayerView, or ArenaJoinFlow.

nodes.name: arena.ts
nodes.summary: Core arena server actions handling full session lifecycle: create/close arenas, join/leave, DM controls for HP/MP/money/attributes/NPC generation, item search and custom item grants, and character participation queries.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses createAdminClient to bypass RLS for all arena-related mutations, consistent with the project pattern.

nodes.name: createArena
nodes.summary: Creates a new arena session with a unique token, retrying up to 5 times on collision.

nodes.name: getMyArenas
nodes.summary: Returns all active arenas for the user, merging DM-owned and participant arenas with participant counts and role labels.

nodes.name: getArenaByToken
nodes.summary: Fetches a full ArenaWithParticipants by token, including joined character data and the caller's role.

nodes.name: dmAddNpcToArena
nodes.summary: Generates a randomized NPC character with calculated HP/MP/defense and inserts it as an arena participant.

nodes.name: dmAdjustMoney
nodes.summary: Allows the DM to give or charge money to a participant, writing both the character balance and a money_transactions record.

nodes.name: companions.ts
nodes.summary: Server actions for managing character companions: listing, adding animals (with optional cost deduction), adding custom companions, updating, marking dead, and removing.

nodes.name: addAnimalCompanion
nodes.summary: Adds an animal companion from the ANIMAL_CONFIGS catalog, optionally deducting the purchase price from the character's money and recording a transaction.

nodes.name: dm.ts
nodes.summary: DM-mode server actions for privileged character edits: updating combat stats/attributes, editing inventory item metadata (improvements, arcanium, label, notes), adjusting money, and deleting inventory entries.

nodes.name: dmEditCharacterStats
nodes.summary: Validates and applies a partial patch to a character's combat stats using Zod and assertOwns ownership check.

nodes.name: inventory.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive inventory server actions covering item addition (with stackability and charge logic), location moves with full equipment-rule enforcement, selling, and bulk operations used by both normal and DM modes.

nodes.name: addToInventory
nodes.summary: Adds an item to a character's inventory, merging stackable duplicates or creating a new record, optionally charging the cost.

nodes.name: moveItem
nodes.summary: Moves an inventory item to a new location, enforcing hand-slot limits, single-shield rule, two-handed restrictions, worn-slot limit, and armour uniqueness (bypassed in DM mode).

nodes.name: levelup.ts
nodes.summary: Server action for saving a level-up, computing new HP/MP via computeLevelUp, recording the history entry, and updating the character record with the new level, class levels, and optionally a raised attribute.

nodes.name: saveLevelUp
nodes.summary: Validates the level-up input with Zod, fetches the character, computes the level result, writes level_ups history and updates the characters table.

nodes.name: portrait.ts
nodes.summary: Server action for uploading a character portrait image to Supabase Storage and updating the character's portrait_url field with a cache-buster timestamp.

nodes.name: uploadPortrait
nodes.summary: Validates file type and size, verifies character ownership, uploads to character-portraits bucket, and stores the public URL.

nodes.name: roll.ts
nodes.summary: Server action for rolling a d20 with modifiers, supporting standard, opposed, and aided roll modes using cryptographically secure random numbers.

nodes.name: rollDice
nodes.summary: Authenticates the caller, validates input with Zod, rolls dice using node:crypto randomInt, computes totals for all three roll modes, and returns a RollResult.

nodes.name: shop-session.ts
nodes.summary: Server actions managing time-limited shop sessions (staged random shop inventories), including get active, create, add items, purchase, and set session stage.

nodes.name: shop.ts
nodes.summary: Server action for fetching or generating a shop's item stock for a given stage and merchant type, persisting the generated inventory to the database for reuse.

nodes.name: getShopStock
nodes.summary: Returns cached shop inventory for the given stage/merchant or generates and persists a new one using generateShopInventory.

nodes.name: default.json
nodes.summary: BabylonJS mesh data for all standard dice (d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, d20, d100) exported from Blender, used as the 3D geometry source for the @3d-dice/dice-box default theme in the assets folder.
nodes.languageNotes: Large JSON blob produced by the Babylon.js Blender exporter; contains raw vertex positions, normals, tangents, UVs, and face indices for each die mesh — not intended for manual editing.

nodes.name: theme.config.json
nodes.summary: Metadata manifest for the @3d-dice/dice-box 'Default Colors' theme, declaring the theme name, version, mesh file reference, PBR material textures (diffuse light/dark, normal, specular), and the list of supported dice types.

nodes.name: default.json
nodes.summary: BabylonJS mesh data for all standard dice (d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, d20, d100) bundled inside the dice-box vendor directory; identical in purpose and structure to the copy under public/assets/themes/default/default.json.
nodes.languageNotes: Duplicate of the assets-folder mesh file; both copies exist to support different loading paths used by the dice-box library at runtime.

nodes.name: theme.config.json
nodes.summary: Metadata manifest for the @3d-dice/dice-box 'Default Colors' theme bundled in the dice-box vendor directory, declaring material textures and supported dice; identical to the copy under public/assets/themes/default/.

nodes.name: dice-box.es.js
nodes.summary: Bundled ES module entry point for the @3d-dice/dice-box library; exports the main DiceBox class (vl) and a helper function (yl/d) that orchestrate 3D dice rolling using BabylonJS inside a Web Worker or OffscreenCanvas context.
nodes.languageNotes: Minified/bundled output — contains multiple internal classes (Zl, vl, M) and functions (Jl, il, nl) that are tree-shaken and renamed. Only vl (DiceBox) and yl (a roll utility) are exported.

nodes.name: world.none.js
nodes.summary: Bundled @3d-dice/dice-box world adapter for non-rendering environments; exports a no-op World class (P) that simulates dice rolls with timeouts and dispatches roll results without any 3D rendering, suitable for server-side or headless usage.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports Dice.js for roll result resolution; uses ES2022 private class fields pattern via WeakMap proxies (compiled output of TypeScript/Babel private fields).

nodes.name: world.offscreen.js
nodes.summary: Bundled @3d-dice/dice-box world adapter that uses OffscreenCanvas to render 3D dice in a Web Worker thread, enabling non-blocking GPU-accelerated dice animations separate from the main browser thread.
nodes.languageNotes: Exports a World class (U) using OffscreenCanvas; the async initialization function N sets up the BabylonJS engine off the main thread, a pattern that avoids UI jank during heavy 3D rendering.

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: React page components, layouts, and interactive UI elements including the character sheet tabs, wizard flows, arena views, shop interface, dice roller, and primitive design-system components.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Next.js API route handlers for auth signup, AI portrait generation, random name generation, and the Supabase auth callback endpoint.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Next.js Server Actions orchestrating all mutations (inventory, companions, leveling, arena, shop, DM mode, rolls, portraits), plus Supabase client factories, auth utilities, custom hooks, and Zod validation schemas.

layers.name: Game Rules Engine
layers.description: Pure domain logic for A Lenda de Ghanor rules: types, HP/MP/defense calculations, leveling system, character classes and races, inventory mechanics, spell and power catalogs, shop generation, character name generation, portrait prompt building, and the guided quiz engine.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Supabase Postgres schema definitions, migration files, and item seed data covering characters, inventory, shop inventories, leveling history, dice rolls, arena sessions, companions, and the full 255-item equipment catalog.

layers.name: Test Layer
layers.description: Vitest unit tests covering the game rules engine (rules, classes, races, origins, skills, spells, powers, names, shop, types) and the guided character creation quiz engine.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Project-level build and runtime configuration files including Next.js, TypeScript, PostCSS, ESLint, Vitest, Supabase local dev settings, game rules JSON, and the PWA manifest.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation including the general README, the AI-agent contributor guide, the arena mode technical design document, and the spell system planning document.

layers.name: Vendor Assets
layers.description: Third-party bundled assets served from public/, including the 3D dice-box WebAssembly/ES module bundle, its web workers, and the dice theme configuration files.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README and CLAUDE.md to understand what Forja de Ghanor is: a fan-made web app for creating and managing characters in the Brazilian TTRPG "A Lenda de Ghanor." These documents lay out the full architecture, database schema, game rule patterns, and contributor checklists — they are the definitive map for navigating the rest of the codebase. Reading them first prevents confusion about conventions like the PC currency unit, RLS client choice, and server action error-return pattern.

tour.title: Application Shell & Entry
tour.description: The root layout (app/layout.tsx) is where Next.js App Router bootstraps the entire application: it attaches PWA metadata, wraps every page in the AppShell server component, and mounts the NavigationProgress and PageTransition client components. AppShell (components/layout/app-shell.tsx) is the authenticated chrome layer — it fetches the Supabase session server-side and conditionally renders the BottomNav, making it the seam between the public and authenticated parts of the app.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router splits layout from pages at the file-system level. A layout.tsx wraps all child routes without re-rendering on navigation, while async server components inside it can directly await database calls — no useEffect, no API fetch, no loading state needed.

tour.title: Domain Types & Rules Engine
tour.description: lib/ghanor/types.ts is the most-imported file in the project (fan-in 26), defining every core domain concept: Attribute, ClassId, RaceId, CharacterBuild, and their display-label maps. Built on top of it, lib/ghanor/rules.ts is the pure game-logic engine (fan-out 20) that computes HP, MP, Defense, skill bonuses, and final attributes from race/origin/mutation modifiers — all without any I/O. Understanding these two files gives you the vocabulary the entire UI and all server actions speak.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript const assertions (`as const`) on arrays derive string-literal union types at compile time. This pattern, used in types.ts for ATTRIBUTES and CLASS_IDS, means adding a new class or race is a single-source change that propagates to all exhaustiveness checks automatically.

tour.title: Game Data Registries
tour.description: Classes, races, and origins are static data registries that the rules engine (Step 3) consults. lib/ghanor/classes.ts holds all 11 playable classes with HP base, MP per level, key attributes, and proficiencies. lib/ghanor/races.ts covers all 7 races including the 10-option aberrant mutation catalog. lib/ghanor/leveling.ts implements the tier progression system and computes HP/MP gained per level — the engine behind the level-up wizard. These files are imported by 8-11 other modules each, making them foundational reference data.

tour.title: Database Schema
tour.description: The authoritative schema lives in supabase/migrations/00000000000000_initial_schema.sql — a single idempotent file that creates every table (characters, items, companions, character_inventory, money_transactions, level_ups, rolls, arenas, arena_participants, shop_inventories, shop_sessions), all enums, helper functions, triggers, indexes, RLS policies, and the Storage bucket for AI portraits. The original progressive migrations in lib/supabase/ show how the schema evolved feature by feature: schema.sql established characters, migrate_equipment.sql introduced the full inventory system, and migrate_leveling_and_rolls.sql added the level-up and dice-roll history tables.
tour.languageLesson: Supabase Row-Level Security (RLS) policies enforce ownership at the database layer, not the application layer. Every table in this schema has policies like `USING (auth.uid() = user_id)`, meaning a misconfigured server action cannot accidentally leak another user's characters.

tour.title: Supabase Client Layer
tour.description: The project uses three distinct Supabase clients, each for a different trust context. lib/supabase/server.ts (fan-in 22, the third most-imported file in the project) creates a cookie-authenticated client for Server Components and Server Actions that respects RLS. lib/supabase/admin.ts (fan-in 13) creates a service-role client that bypasses RLS — used only for joins against the items catalog where RLS would block cross-user reads. lib/supabase/client.ts handles browser-side subscriptions, notably used by the Arena's real-time feature.
tour.languageLesson: The rule 'use admin only when you must join against a table you don't own' is enforced by convention, not types. In Supabase, the service role key must never be exposed to the browser — the admin client is instantiated only inside `app/actions/` and `app/api/` files that run exclusively on the server.

tour.title: Character Creation Wizard
tour.description: New characters are created through two paths, both living under app/characters/new/. The manual path renders the CharacterWizard (components/wizard/character-wizard.tsx, fan-out 16), an 8-step form that walks through name, attributes, race, class, origin, skills, spells, and starter equipment. All cross-step draft state is managed by a Zustand store (components/wizard/store.ts). On submission, app/characters/actions.ts (fan-out 16) persists the character, grants the starter kit, and handles both full and guided creation paths.
tour.languageLesson: Zustand stores in Next.js App Router must not be module-level singletons when used with SSR — this project scopes the store to the wizard component tree to avoid sharing state between different users' server renders. The store is initialized fresh for each wizard session.

tour.title: Guided Quiz Creation
tour.description: The second creation path is a personality quiz aimed at players new to TTRPGs. lib/ghanor/quiz.ts defines 18 questions across three acts, each answer scoring a mix of attributes, class affinities, and personality tags. lib/ghanor/quiz-engine.ts aggregates those scores into a ranked class recommendation and suggested origin. The GuidedCreationWizard (components/wizard/guided/GuidedCreationWizard.tsx) orchestrates the six quiz screens: welcome, race selection, questions, act transitions, final touches, and the result screen where the player confirms and saves.

tour.title: Character Sheet
tour.description: Once created, a character's home is app/characters/[id]/page.tsx, which parallelizes six Supabase fetches (character, level history, inventory, transactions, catalog, companions) before rendering the root CharacterSheet component. CharacterSheet (components/character-sheet/character-sheet.tsx, fan-out 29 — the highest in the project) orchestrates all sub-tabs: the main stat sheet, inventory, and companions. It also owns HP/MP tracking via localStorage, the DM-mode toggle, portrait upload, and the 3D dice roll dialog.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js Server Components can use `Promise.all` to fire multiple Supabase queries in parallel, cutting total page load time from the sum of each query's latency to the latency of the slowest single query. The character sheet page uses this pattern for all six data sources.

tour.title: Inventory System
tour.description: The inventory system is one of the most complex features in the app. components/character-sheet/inventory-tab.tsx (fan-out 8) renders four sub-tabs (equipped, carried, stored, history) and handles item movement, selling, and DM-mode editing. Behind it, app/actions/inventory.ts enforces all equipment rules from lib/ghanor/inventory.ts: hand-slot limits, the four-item worn limit, cosmetic item exceptions, carry capacity with encumbrance zones, and the 50%-of-price sell calculation. The item catalog (255 items across 16 categories) is seeded by three idempotent SQL files.

tour.title: Arena Mode
tour.description: Arena Mode enables shared real-time combat sessions between a Dungeon Master and players. ARENA_MODE_PLAN.md describes the design: the DM creates a session and shares a token; players join by entering the token and selecting a character. app/actions/arena.ts (fan-in 9, fan-out 14) handles the full session lifecycle — create, join, leave, close, and all DM controls for adjusting HP/MP, attributes, money, and items. ArenaDashboard (components/arena/ArenaDashboard.tsx) uses a Supabase Realtime subscription on the arena_participants table so HP/MP changes propagate live to all connected browsers.
tour.languageLesson: Supabase Realtime uses PostgreSQL's logical replication to broadcast row changes to subscribed clients via WebSocket. The arena tables are explicitly added to the `supabase_realtime` publication, meaning only those tables stream changes — other tables remain private and efficient.

tour.title: Testing & Data Validation
tour.description: The game logic layer is thoroughly tested with Vitest. tests/ghanor/rules.test.ts verifies HP/MP calculations across all classes and races; lib/ghanor/__tests__/quiz.test.ts confirms each of the 11 classes wins when given ideal answers; lib/ghanor/inventory.test.ts covers every currency, encumbrance, and equipment rule. On the data side, scripts/validate-seed.js runs as a pre-build step to verify slug format, category validity, weapon field completeness, and exact per-category item counts — the build fails if the 255-item catalog drifts from expectations.
tour.languageLesson: Vitest's `jsdom` environment lets the test suite run DOM-dependent TypeScript in Node.js without a browser. Path aliases (`@/*`) in vitest.config.ts mirror tsconfig.json exactly, so the same import paths used in production code work seamlessly in tests.
